title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning for the Braves, defeating the Red Sox 3-2.
The Red Sox fell to 18-26 on the season after losing to the Braves in extra innings.
Boston's starter Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs in five solid innings, striking out six without walks.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' reunion with Stefon Diggs seen as unlikely due to impending A.J. Brown trade.
Diggs, interested in returning, posted 1,045 yards with Patriots but faces competition from new acquisitions.
Trade for A.J. Brown would allocate Patriots' funds, limiting option for Diggs' return.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester on Tuesday around 1 p.m.
The Boston Police Department describes him as a Black male, 5'8", with brown eyes and black hair.
Authorities urge anyone with information to call 911 or contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on May 10.
The victims, both 42, were shot outside a Kingsdale Street home; the woman suffered life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder; he's held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox pitchers, including Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi, maintain a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Boston's former players like Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts, continue to thrive for other teams in 2026.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs, highlighting impactful players the Red Sox have let go.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox player Roman Anthony has been out since May 7 with a right wrist sprain.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch, signaling progress in his recovery.
Anthony is hopeful to resume swinging soon, depending on his recovery from wrist injury.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep drove into the BrightPath daycare in Norwood after the driver accidentally accelerated on Friday morning.
No injuries were reported, but the daycare was temporarily closed to assess structural damage.
The crash happened while the driver was dropping off a passenger and failed to put the vehicle in park.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 season with a 10-7 record, worse than their 14-3 record in 2025.
The Patriots' challenging 2026 schedule includes opponents like the Seahawks, Broncos, and Bears, contributing to lower win predictions.
Some analysts, like Brian Hines, are more optimistic, projecting a 12-5 record for the Patriots in the 2026 season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Mars Attacks!' is streaming free on YouTube and Plex, featuring Martin Short in a comedic role.
'Marty, Life is Short,' a documentary on Martin Short, is now available on Netflix.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2, hosted by Colin Jost, is streaming on Netflix.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu's $4.9 billion budget faces potential rejection from Boston City Council members seeking increased spending.
Council members can alter but not increase overall budget; decision deadline is June 10, with possible mayoral veto.
City faces a $50 million deficit, attributed to costs like snow removal and police overtime, as budget debates continue.
